item.title: Chinese Satellites Over Mideast Battlefield Put U.S. on Edge
item.description: Chinese satellite imagery of the conflict zone is potentially offering battlefield guidance to Iran and other U.S. adversaries.

item.title: Rules of Origin Set Up U.S.-China Clash in Asia
item.description: Plus, factory owners worry about having to scrap decades-old supplier ties.

item.title: Opinion | Trump’s Last Chance to Fix the Navy
item.description: Pentagon infighting is hurting the chance to rebuild the sea arsenal.

item.title: Secretive Shipments of Iranian Oil to China Are Under Threat by U.S.
item.description: Iran, China and an array of middlemen have evaded U.S. sanctions for years by shipping oil on aging tankers with opaque records.

item.title: Pull of the American Dream Weakens for China’s Best and Brightest
item.description: U.S. immigration hurdles and fear of crime, amplified by state media, have become reasons for Chinese talent to stay home.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.

item.title: China’s Cyberspying Targets Western Defense Industry, Dutch Intel Chief Says
item.description: A military intelligence report warned that Beijing poses a growing threat to Europe alongside Russia and that the two countries’ increasing cooperation compounds the danger.

item.title: Seized Iranian-Flagged Ship Was Part of Fleet That Frequented China
item.description: The cargo ship seized by U.S. forces in the Gulf of Oman belongs to a subsidiary of a sanctioned Iranian state-owned group.

item.title: Chinese Satellites Over Mideast Battlefield Put U.S. on Edge
item.description: Chinese satellite imagery of the conflict zone is potentially offering battlefield guidance to Iran and other U.S. adversaries.
